test_name: Identify all accessible API endpoints and verify they are documented.
Enumerate HTTP methods allowed per endpoint and flag dangerous methods (TRACE, CONNECT).
Ensure all API endpoints are protected by proper authentication mechanisms.
Verify that authentication tokens are validated server-side and not merely checked client-side.
Test for broken object-level authorization — access another user's objects by manipulating IDs.
Fuzz parameters for SQL injection and Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) payloads.
Test for path traversal vulnerabilities in file-serving or path-parameter endpoints.
Test for Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) by injecting internal URLs into URL parameters.
Scan response bodies for regex-detectable sensitive data (PII, credentials, tokens, API keys).
Use AI to detect subtle or contextually sensitive data exposure not caught by static patterns.
Verify that responses do not return excessive data fields beyond what the client requested.
Audit CORS policy for wildcard origins or reflected origins with credentials enabled.
Verify presence and correct configuration of HTTP security headers (HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options).
Send malformed requests to trigger application errors and check for stack trace or path disclosure.
Send a burst of parallel requests to verify the API enforces rate limiting (429 Too Many Requests).
Verify that rate limiting applies per-user and cannot be bypassed by rotating IP headers (X-Forwarded-For).
Test for mass assignment vulnerabilities by sending unexpected or privileged parameters in request bodies.
Verify that write operations reject or ignore unexpected fields not in the API schema.
Test for transaction limit bypass — attempt to exceed defined thresholds through repeated or split operations.
Verify workflow integrity — test that multi-step processes cannot be interrupted or skipped.
Test for negative value or boundary manipulation in numeric inputs (quantities, prices, scores).
Verify WebSocket handshake enforces authentication (token in header or subprotocol).
Test WebSocket messages for injection vulnerabilities and verify message-level authorization.
Verify that third-party webhook endpoints validate incoming payloads using signatures or secrets.
Ensure that error responses from third-party integrations do not leak upstream system details.
Verify that API keys and secrets are not present in response bodies or headers.
Confirm that staging/debug endpoints are not exposed in the production environment.
